title: rx_thread re-enters lora_recv immediately on non-EAGAIN errors
description:
In lora_l2.c rx_thread() (lines 152-158), when lora_recv() returns a negative value other than -EAGAIN, the error is logged but the loop immediately retries without any delay.

Trigger: Persistent LoRa driver error (e.g., -EIO from misconfigured SPI, hardware failure, or driver bug).

Bad behavior: Tight retry loop floods logs with 'LoRa RX error: -X' messages at maximum rate, starving other threads (priority 7 is relatively high), and potentially masking the root cause in the log buffer. On battery-powered devices, this also drains power rapidly.

Consider: exponential backoff on repeated non-EAGAIN errors, or stopping after N consecutive failures.

Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.c lines 152-158
close_reason: Added 1-second backoff delay after non-EAGAIN lora_recv errors to prevent log flooding and CPU starvation
